characters.name: a cautious inventor
characters.wish: to fix a problem without making a fuss

characters.name: a brave class hamster
characters.trait: keeps a notebook under the sawdust
characters.wish: to prove small things can lead

characters.name: a mapmaker who gets lost
characters.wish: to find the one path nobody labeled

characters.name: a librarian with a weather jar
a shy dragon made of chalk
characters.wish: to be remembered after the rain

characters.name: a time-travelling lunch monitor
characters.wish: to stop a very polite disaster

characters.name: a detective with mismatched socks
characters.wish: to solve the mystery before assembly

characters.name: a gardener who measures moonlight
characters.wish: to help something grow in the right place

characters.name: a museum guide who whispers to fossils
characters.wish: to make the past feel close

characters.name: a poet who repairs bicycles
characters.wish: to turn a problem into a pattern

characters.name: a class captain with a pocket telescope

settings.name: the school library
settings.texture: the shelves hum when nobody is reading

settings.name: the playground after rain
settings.texture: the puddles show places that are not there

settings.name: the art cupboard
settings.texture: every paintbrush remembers a different ocean

settings.name: the science table
settings.texture: there are magnets, labels, and one suspicious seed tray

settings.name: the lost-and-found box
settings.texture: it is full of gloves, keys, and unfinished stories

settings.name: the moonlit classroom
settings.texture: the desks are arranged like a tiny town

settings.name: the museum coach trip
settings.texture: the windows are fogged with excited fingerprints

settings.name: the garden behind the sports hall
settings.texture: there are bees, beanpoles, and a gate with no handle

settings.name: the corridor timeline
settings.texture: each display board shows a different year

settings.name: the breakfast club table
settings.texture: toast crumbs form a map nobody planned

settings.name: the reading corner tent
settings.texture: torchlight makes shadows look like punctuation

objects.name: a runaway umbrella
objects.twist: opens whenever someone tells the truth

objects.name: a pencil that writes questions
objects.twist: never gives the same question twice

objects.name: a compass that points to kindness
a glass marble full of weather
a lunchbox with a secret compartment
a paper crown
objects.twist: makes rules rhyme for one minute

objects.name: a tiny brass bell
objects.twist: rings when a forgotten idea returns

objects.name: a seed packet with no label
objects.twist: grows exactly what the story needs

objects.name: a bookmark shaped like a door
objects.twist: appears on the page before the answer

objects.name: a magnifying glass with a cold handle
a button from an old coat
objects.twist: remembers every journey it has taken

objects.name: a ruler that measures courage
objects.twist: gets longer when someone tells the truth kindly

constraints: include one question in the first paragraph
show a choice without explaining it
make the object important by the end
include one sound, one texture, and one surprise
start with a normal moment that turns strange
make the setting feel alive
use dialogue to reveal the problem
end with a new mystery
extensions: draw the scene and label three important details
write the next five lines of dialogue
make a word bank of six useful words
create a map of the setting
write a second ending from another character's view
turn the idea into a two-minute group performance
list three questions a reader might ask
design the object and explain its rules
discussion_questions: What would change if the character made the opposite choice?
Which detail gives the best clue about the setting?
How could the class solve the problem together?
What does the object seem to want?
Which word in the prompt feels most important?
What would make the ending more surprising but still fair?

constraints: include one vivid setting detail
show a feeling through an action
use one line of dialogue
end with a question
extensions: underline the sentence that best creates curiosity
write a second opening in a different mood
swap the narrator and rewrite three sentences

constraints: include one observation and one prediction
separate what is seen from what is guessed
describe a fair test the class could try
include one cause and one effect
extensions: draw a labelled diagram of the investigation
write one prediction and one result
make a table with three observations

constraints: include one artefact as evidence
show how life is different from today
put three events in order
include one source that might be unreliable
extensions: write a museum label for the object
make a three-event timeline
ask one question a historian would investigate

constraints: show the feeling through body language
include a kind repair after a mistake
give two characters different points of view
include one helpful question
extensions: write what the character could say next
draw a feelings thermometer for the scene
list two kind choices and one unhelpful choice

constraints: include one obstacle and one clever solution
make the map slightly wrong
show teamwork without making it too easy
end with a new route opening
extensions: draw the route and mark the turning point
write the next obstacle
invent a rule for travelling safely

constraints: keep the joke kind
make one normal rule become silly
include a serious character taking nonsense seriously
end with a harmless surprise
extensions: write the funniest line again in a calmer voice
invent three rules for the silly object
draw the moment just before everything goes wrong

constraints: include three clues and one false lead
make the answer possible from the evidence
let a tiny detail matter later
end before the answer is fully known
extensions: circle the clue that matters most
write a suspect list with motives
draw the scene and mark where each clue was found
